frameworks.name: noisy template matching
frameworks.one_line: ensemble-averaged template compared item-by-item under computational noise

frameworks.name: approximate number system (ANS)
frameworks.one_line: mechanistic account of ANS numerosity estimation

frameworks.name: ensemble coding
frameworks.one_line: template formed via ensemble averaging across array items

core_question: What computational mechanism underlies approximate numerosity estimation in the ANS?
key_assumptions: observers form a template via ensemble averaging of array items
each item is compared to the template through a matching process
encoding mechanism is shared across magnitudes
manipulations: stimulus factors hypothesized to modulate the coherence illusion
key_findings: model reproduces Weber's law for numerosity
model accounts for systematic numerosity underestimation
model accounts for the coherence illusion
two novel predictions about coherence-illusion modulators confirmed empirically
interpretations: noisy template matching is a plausible mechanistic substrate for the ANS
core psychophysical signatures of number perception emerge from limited, noisy comparison processes
